dialogueChunks.intro: The guild council reviewed the evidence for four hours. When they emerged, the master's name had been struck from every register. Thirty years of reputation, gone in an afternoon.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Three former apprentices have been formally cleared of the espionage charges. {target_npc} says one of them wept when they heard. They'd been living under that shadow for eight years. {quest_ally} is overseeing the restitution process: every apprentice who was cheated will receive compensation. The master has been barred from the profession permanently.
dialogueChunks.outro: Finding the truth cost the apprentice who started this everything they believed about their mentor, {player_name}. But it gave three innocent people their names back. Some truths are worth the price of disillusionment.

playerResponses.text: The apprentice showed more integrity than the master ever did.
Eight years of false disgrace. Make sure they're truly made whole.

dialogueChunks.intro: The evidence from the battlefield was undeniable. The weapon positions, the barricade layout: they told a story completely different from the one carved into the memorial.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The council removed the false hero from their position. The memorial is being recarved with the names of the actual defenders. {target_npc} found a survivor from the real fight, living quietly in {location}, who confirmed everything. {quest_ally} says the settlement is in shock, but it's the clean kind of shock: the kind that comes before honest rebuilding.
dialogueChunks.outro: It's painful to learn your hero was a fraud, {player_name}. But the real heroes, the ones who died on that field, deserve their names on the stone. You made sure the right story gets told from now on.

playerResponses.text: The real defenders waited a long time for their due. Better late than never.
A settlement that can face its own lies is stronger for it.
Watch for the next person who tells you what you want to hear.

dialogueChunks.intro: The administrator was caught at the border with two packed wagons and a new identity. The full records are now in the hands of the regional authority.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The accounting is devastating. Over three years, enough money to house and feed every displaced family was diverted to personal luxury. {target_npc} says the recovered assets will be redistributed to the families who were owed relief. {quest_ally} is coordinating with neighboring settlements to distribute the funds where they were originally promised.
dialogueChunks.outro: The most trusted person in the settlement turned out to be its worst predator, {player_name}. The families who suffered will finally receive what was promised. It doesn't undo the months they spent without shelter, but it's the closest thing to justice that's possible.

playerResponses.text: Justice delayed is painful, but it still matters. Well done.
Build oversight into the next relief fund. Trust alone isn't enough.
